intro2: From a complete gut renovation to a targeted cabinet and countertop refresh, we handle every aspect of the project: design consultation, demolition, plumbing and electrical coordination, custom cabinetry, tile work, and final finishing — with no subcontracting surprises.
metaTitle: Kitchen Renovation Ontario | Top Design Renovation — Free Estimate
metaDesc: Professional kitchen renovation in Ontario. Custom designs, quality materials & skilled tradespeople. Full project management from design to finish. Free estimate.
whyUs: Custom cabinetry built to your exact specifications

benefits.title: Custom Design
benefits.desc: We work with you to design a layout that maximises storage, flow, and style — from open-concept to galley kitchens.

benefits.title: Premium Materials
benefits.desc: We source only top-rated cabinets, countertops, tiles, and fixtures — built to look stunning and stand up to daily use.

benefits.title: 5-Year Warranty
benefits.desc: All our kitchen renovations are backed by a 5-year workmanship warranty. We stand behind every cabinet door and tile we install.

faqs.q: How much does a kitchen renovation cost in Ontario?
faqs.a: Kitchen renovations in Ontario typically range from $20,000 to $80,000 depending on the scope. A basic cabinet and countertop refresh starts around $15,000–$25,000, while a full gut renovation with custom cabinets and premium finishes runs $40,000–$80,000. We provide a detailed written estimate before any work begins.

faqs.q: How long does a kitchen renovation take?
faqs.a: Most kitchen renovations take 4–8 weeks from demo to final walkthrough. A smaller refresh (countertops + hardware) can be completed in 1–2 weeks. We provide a detailed timeline at the start of your project.

faqs.q: Do I need permits for a kitchen renovation in Ontario?
faqs.a: Most cosmetic kitchen renovations don't require permits. However, if the scope includes moving walls, relocating plumbing, or upgrading electrical panels, permits are required. We handle all permit applications on your behalf.

faqs.q: Can I stay in my home during the renovation?
faqs.a: Yes — most clients remain in their homes during kitchen renovations. We contain the work area, maintain clean pathways, and work Monday to Friday to minimise disruption to your routine.

faqs.q: How much does a bathroom renovation cost in Ontario?
faqs.a: Bathroom renovations in Ontario range from $10,000 for a basic refresh to $40,000+ for a full primary bathroom with custom tile and premium fixtures. A standard main-floor bathroom renovation typically costs $15,000–$25,000.

faqs.q: How long does a bathroom renovation take?
faqs.a: A standard bathroom renovation takes 2–4 weeks. Larger ensuite renovations can take 4–6 weeks. Waterproofing and tile setting require proper cure times, which we build into the schedule.

faqs.q: Can I choose my own tiles and fixtures?
faqs.a: Absolutely. We work with multiple suppliers and can source materials from your preferred retailer or showroom. We can also guide you to our trusted suppliers for the best selection and pricing.

faqs.q: Do you offer accessible bathroom renovations?
faqs.a: Yes — we specialize in barrier-free and accessible bathroom designs including walk-in showers with zero thresholds, grab bars, comfort-height toilets, and wider doorways for mobility aid access.

title: Basement Renovation

intro2: We handle every aspect: waterproofing assessment, framing, insulation, electrical, drywall, flooring, pot lights, and full finishing. We also handle all necessary building permits with the city.
metaTitle: Basement Renovation Ontario | Top Design Renovation — Free Estimate
metaDesc: Convert your unfinished basement into beautiful living space. Expert basement renovations in Ontario — in-law suites, home offices, media rooms. Free estimate.
whyUs: Building permit application handled by our team

benefits.title: Add Living Space
benefits.desc: A finished basement can add 500–1,500 sq ft of liveable space to your home — worth significantly more than the renovation cost.

benefits.title: Generate Rental Income
benefits.desc: A legal secondary suite or in-law apartment can generate $1,500–$2,500/month in rental income, dramatically improving your home's ROI.

benefits.title: Permit-Compliant
benefits.desc: Every basement renovation we complete meets current Ontario building code requirements. We handle all permits and inspections.

faqs.q: How much does a basement renovation cost in Ontario?
faqs.a: Basic basement finishing in Ontario starts around $30–$60 per sq ft. A 1,000 sq ft basement typically costs $35,000–$60,000 for a standard finish. A full in-law suite with kitchen and bathroom runs $50,000–$90,000.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to finish my basement in Ontario?
faqs.a: Yes. Finishing or renovating a basement requires a building permit in most Ontario municipalities. We apply for permits on your behalf and manage all inspections throughout the project.

faqs.q: How do you prevent basement moisture issues?
faqs.a: We start every basement renovation with a moisture assessment. Depending on findings, we may recommend interior drainage membranes, sump pump upgrades, or exterior waterproofing. Proper moisture control is done before any framing begins.

faqs.q: Can a finished basement be used as a rental unit?
faqs.a: Yes, but it must meet specific legal requirements including egress windows, fire separation, separate entrance, and HVAC. We are experienced in building legal secondary suites that comply with municipal requirements.

title: Interior & Exterior Painting
tagline: A fresh coat of paint is the fastest way to transform a space.
intro1: Professional painting is about far more than colour. It's proper preparation, the right primer, expert application, and clean edges that make the difference between a DIY look and a showroom finish. Our Ontario painting team delivers that showroom finish every time.
intro2: We handle interior and exterior painting for homes of all sizes — from a single accent wall to a full house repaint inside and out. We use only premium paints (Benjamin Moore, Sherwin-Williams) and protect all surfaces before we ever open a can.
metaTitle: Interior & Exterior Painting Ontario | Top Design Renovation — Free Estimate
metaDesc: Professional interior & exterior painting in Ontario. Premium paints, meticulous prep, clean results. Residential & commercial painting. Free estimate.
whyUs: Benjamin Moore & Sherwin-Williams certified applicators

benefits.title: Colour Consultation

benefits.title: Meticulous Prep
benefits.desc: We fill cracks, sand surfaces, apply the correct primer, and tape every edge. Proper preparation is what separates a lasting paint job from one that peels within a year.

benefits.title: Exterior Protection
benefits.desc: Our exterior coatings protect against Ontario's freeze-thaw cycles, UV damage, and moisture. We prep and prime before applying two full coats.

faqs.q: How much does interior painting cost in Ontario?
faqs.a: Interior painting in Ontario typically costs $2–$4 per sq ft depending on prep work required and number of rooms. A standard 2,000 sq ft home runs $4,000–$8,000 for a full interior repaint.

faqs.q: How long does a full interior paint job take?
faqs.a: A full interior repaint of an average home (3 bedrooms) takes 3–5 days. We work efficiently to minimise disruption while never rushing the prep work.

faqs.q: Can you paint while I'm living in the house?
faqs.a: Yes — we work room by room and use low-VOC paints to minimise fumes. Furniture is moved and protected before painting begins and returned at the end of each day.

faqs.q: What exterior painting prep do you do?
faqs.a: All exterior surfaces are pressure washed, scraped, sanded, primed, and caulked before painting. This prep work is what ensures the paint bonds properly and lasts for years.

intro2: We work with all exterior cladding types — vinyl, fibre cement, engineered wood, and stucco — and coordinate all associated work including eavestroughs, windows, insulation upgrades, and exterior painting.
metaTitle: Exterior Renovation Ontario | Top Design Renovation — Free Estimate

benefits.title: Curb Appeal
benefits.desc: A new exterior immediately transforms how your home looks and feels — from the street and from the inside. Neighbours notice, and buyers pay a premium.

benefits.title: Weather Protection
benefits.desc: Quality siding and window installation protects your home from Ontario's extreme temperature swings, heavy rain, and ice. We install with proper moisture barriers and flashing.

benefits.title: Energy Savings
benefits.desc: Exterior renovations are the ideal time to upgrade wall insulation. We can improve your home's thermal envelope and reduce heating and cooling costs.

faqs.q: How much does exterior siding replacement cost in Ontario?
faqs.a: Vinyl siding replacement typically costs $8,000–$20,000 for an average home. Fibre cement (Hardie Board) runs $15,000–$35,000. Prices vary based on home size, cladding type, and condition of existing sheathing.

faqs.q: What's the best siding material for Ontario climate?
faqs.a: Fibre cement (James Hardie) is the top choice for Ontario — it handles freeze-thaw cycles, doesn't rot, and holds paint exceptionally well. Premium vinyl is also an excellent value option with modern profiles that look like real wood.

faqs.q: Should I replace windows at the same time as siding?
faqs.a: If your windows are more than 15 years old, doing both simultaneously is significantly more cost-effective — the siding work involves opening up walls around window frames anyway.

faqs.q: How long does an exterior renovation take?
faqs.a: Full exterior siding replacement on an average home takes 1–2 weeks. Combined siding + windows + trim projects run 2–4 weeks.

title: Roof Replacement

intro2: We are certified installers for GAF, CertainTeed, and Owens Corning roofing systems. Every replacement includes a full tear-off, deck inspection, ice-and-water shield on all eaves, proper ventilation assessment, and clean-up with a magnetic nail sweep of your property.
metaTitle: Roof Replacement Ontario | Top Design Renovation — Free Estimate
metaDesc: Expert roof replacement in Ontario. GAF, CertainTeed & Owens Corning certified installers. Full tear-off, 25-year shingle warranty. Free roof estimate.

faqs.q: How much does a roof replacement cost in Ontario?
faqs.a: Roof replacements in Ontario typically cost $8,000–$20,000 for a standard residential home. Price depends on roof size, pitch, existing layers, and shingle grade. We provide a detailed written quote after a free inspection.

faqs.q: How long does a roof replacement take?
faqs.a: Most residential roof replacements are completed in 1–2 days. Larger or more complex roofs may take 2–3 days. Weather permitting, we complete the project without leaving your roof exposed overnight.

faqs.q: How do I know if I need a full replacement vs. repairs?
faqs.a: If your roof is over 20–25 years old, has multiple layers, or shows widespread granule loss, curling, or cracking, a full replacement is typically more cost-effective than ongoing repairs. We'll give you an honest assessment.

faqs.q: What shingle brands do you recommend?
faqs.a: We recommend GAF Timberline HDZ, CertainTeed Landmark, and Owens Corning Duration shingles — all of which offer excellent durability for Ontario's climate, strong wind ratings, and attractive aesthetics.

title: Roof Repairs
tagline: Stop the leak before it becomes a disaster.
intro1: A small roof leak ignored becomes a major structural problem quickly. Our Ontario roof repair team responds fast — diagnosing the true source of the leak and fixing it properly the first time, not just patching it temporarily.
intro2: We repair all types of roofing issues: missing shingles, damaged flashing around chimneys and vents, ice dam damage, ridge cap failures, and pipe collar leaks. All repairs come with a written warranty.
metaTitle: Roof Repairs Ontario | Top Design Renovation — Fast Response
metaDesc: Fast, reliable roof repairs in Ontario. Leaks, missing shingles, damaged flashing & ice dam damage. Written warranty on all repairs. Free estimate.
whyUs: Fast response — same-week appointments available

benefits.title: Fast Response
benefits.desc: Roof leaks are emergencies. We offer same-week appointments and emergency tarping to protect your home while we arrange a permanent repair.

benefits.title: True Diagnosis
benefits.desc: We find where water is actually entering — which is often not directly above where it's showing inside. We fix the source, not just the symptom.

benefits.title: Written Warranty
benefits.desc: All our roof repair work comes with a written labour warranty so you have peace of mind that the fix will hold.

faqs.q: How much do roof repairs cost in Ontario?
faqs.a: Minor roof repairs (missing shingles, small flashing repairs) typically cost $300–$800. More extensive repairs involving multiple areas or structural damage run $1,000–$3,500. We provide a written estimate before any work begins.

faqs.q: How quickly can you respond to a roof leak?
faqs.a: We typically offer same-week appointments for roof repairs. For active leaks during rain events, we can provide emergency tarping within 24 hours to prevent further water damage.

faqs.q: Should I repair or replace my roof?
faqs.a: If your roof is under 15 years old and damage is isolated, a repair is usually the right call. If it's over 20 years old or damage is widespread, a full replacement is often more economical in the long run. We'll give you an honest recommendation.

faqs.q: What causes most roof leaks?
faqs.a: The most common sources of roof leaks in Ontario are deteriorated flashing around chimneys and skylights, failed pipe boot collars, ice dam damage, and missing or lifted shingles. We inspect and address all of these.

title: Metal Roofing
tagline: The last roof you'll ever need to install.
intro1: Metal roofing is the premium choice for Ontario homeowners who want a roof that lasts 40–70 years with minimal maintenance. Standing seam and exposed fastener metal panels are energy-efficient, lightweight, and available in a wide range of colours.
intro2: We are experienced in both exposed fastener steel roofing and concealed fastener standing seam systems. Our metal roof installations include proper underlayment, closed-cell insulation options, and trim and flashing work that matches the clean aesthetic of the panels.
metaTitle: Metal Roofing Ontario | Top Design Renovation — Standing Seam & Steel
metaDesc: Expert metal roofing installation in Ontario. Standing seam & steel roofing systems. 40–70 year lifespan. Energy-efficient, low-maintenance. Free estimate.
whyUs: Standing seam & exposed fastener metal systems

benefits.title: 50-Year Lifespan
benefits.desc: Metal roofing lasts 2–3× longer than asphalt shingles. Properly installed steel or aluminum roofing can last 40–70 years with minimal maintenance.

benefits.title: Energy Efficiency
benefits.desc: Metal roofs reflect solar radiation, reducing summer cooling costs by up to 25%. Many products are Energy Star rated and qualify for energy efficiency credits.

benefits.title: Snow Performance
benefits.desc: Metal panels shed snow efficiently, reducing the structural load on your roof deck and minimising ice dam formation during Ontario winters.

faqs.q: How much does metal roofing cost in Ontario?
faqs.a: Metal roofing costs $15,000–$35,000 for a standard residential home, depending on panel type and roof complexity. While higher upfront than asphalt, the 50+ year lifespan makes it the most cost-effective choice long-term.

faqs.q: Is metal roofing loud in the rain?
faqs.a: Modern metal roofing installed over solid sheathing with proper underlayment is no louder than asphalt shingles. The structure beneath the panels absorbs sound effectively.

faqs.q: What metal roofing systems do you install?
faqs.a: We install exposed fastener corrugated and R-panel systems (most economical), snap-lock standing seam (mid-range), and mechanical seam standing seam (premium). We'll recommend the best system for your roof geometry and budget.

faqs.q: Can metal roofing be installed over existing shingles?
faqs.a: Yes, in many cases metal panels can be installed over existing asphalt shingles, saving tear-off costs. We assess the existing deck condition before recommending this approach.

faqs.q: How much does siding replacement cost in Ontario?
faqs.a: Vinyl siding replacement costs $12–$18 per sq ft installed. Fibre cement (James Hardie) costs $18–$28 per sq ft. An average 2,000 sq ft home runs $15,000–$35,000 depending on material and home complexity.

faqs.q: What is the best siding for Ontario climate?
faqs.a: James Hardie fibre cement is our top recommendation for Ontario — it's dimensionally stable through freeze-thaw cycles, resists moisture, and holds its painted finish for 15+ years. Premium vinyl is also excellent for cold climates with modern insulated profiles.

faqs.q: How long does siding installation take?
faqs.a: Siding installation on an average home takes 1–2 weeks. Larger homes or those requiring significant sheathing repair may take 2–3 weeks.

faqs.q: Should I replace my soffits and eavestroughs at the same time?
faqs.a: If your soffits or eavestroughs are original or showing wear, combining the work saves mobilisation costs and ensures a cohesive finished look.

title: Soffit & Fascia

intro1: Soffits and fascia protect your roof's edge and overhang from water, pests, and rot — while giving your home's exterior a clean, finished appearance. When they fail, water enters your wall assembly and attic, causing costly damage.
intro2: We replace deteriorated wood soffits and fascia with low-maintenance aluminum or vinyl cladding that never rots, requires no painting, and is available in colours to match your siding and trim.
metaTitle: Soffit & Fascia Ontario | Top Design Renovation — Free Estimate
metaDesc: Expert soffit & fascia installation in Ontario. Aluminum & vinyl replacement for wood rot protection. Improves ventilation & curb appeal. Free estimate.
whyUs: Aluminum & vinyl — never rots, never needs painting

benefits.title: Pest Prevention
benefits.desc: Intact soffit panels prevent birds, squirrels, and insects from entering your attic. We seal all gaps and install proper vented panels.

benefits.title: Attic Ventilation
benefits.desc: Vented soffit panels are critical for proper attic airflow, which extends roof shingle life and prevents ice dams in winter.

benefits.title: Rot Protection
benefits.desc: Aluminum and vinyl soffit and fascia eliminate the rot, peeling paint, and wasp nest issues that plague original wood installations.

faqs.q: How much does soffit and fascia replacement cost?
faqs.a: Soffit and fascia replacement typically costs $3,000–$8,000 for an average home. Price depends on linear footage, condition of existing wood substrate, and whether eavestroughs are being replaced simultaneously.

faqs.q: How do I know if my soffit and fascia need replacement?
faqs.a: Signs include peeling or flaking paint, soft spots, visible rot, water stains on the siding below the roofline, wasps or birds nesting in gaps, or visible daylight through soffit panels.

faqs.q: Can you match my existing house colours?
faqs.a: Yes — aluminum soffit and fascia are available in dozens of standard colours and can often be custom powder-coated to match virtually any existing exterior colour scheme.

faqs.q: Should I replace eavestroughs at the same time?
faqs.a: If eavestroughs are being removed for soffit and fascia work anyway, combining the replacement saves significant labour cost and ensures a clean, consistent look.

title: Eavestrough & Gutter Guards
tagline: Protect your foundation from water damage.
intro1: Eavestroughs direct roof runoff away from your foundation — without them, water pools against your home and causes basement leaks, foundation erosion, and landscape damage. We install seamless aluminum eavestroughs fabricated on-site for a perfect, leak-free fit.
intro2: We also install gutter guards that prevent leaf and debris buildup, eliminating twice-yearly cleaning. All our eavestrough systems include proper slope, downspout sizing, and extensions to move water well away from your foundation.
metaTitle: Eavestrough & Gutter Guards Ontario | Top Design Renovation — Free Estimate
metaDesc: Seamless aluminum eavestrough installation & gutter guard systems in Ontario. Protect your foundation from water damage. Free estimate.
whyUs: Seamless aluminum — fabricated on-site, no joints to leak

benefits.title: Foundation Protection
benefits.desc: Properly sized eavestroughs with adequate downspouts move thousands of litres of water away from your foundation every year.

benefits.title: Seamless Construction
benefits.desc: Our eavestroughs are fabricated on-site in continuous runs — no joints means no leaks, no sagging, and a cleaner aesthetic.

benefits.title: Gutter Guards
benefits.desc: Quality micro-mesh gutter guards prevent leaves and debris from entering your gutters while allowing full water flow — eliminating annual cleaning.

faqs.q: How much does eavestrough installation cost?
faqs.a: Seamless aluminum eavestrough installation typically costs $8–$15 per linear foot installed. A full eavestrough replacement on an average home runs $2,000–$5,000. Gutter guard installation adds $10–$20 per linear foot.

faqs.q: When should I replace my eavestroughs?
faqs.a: Replace eavestroughs when you see sagging sections, visible rust or holes, pulling away from the fascia, water overflowing during normal rain, or foundation damp issues that correlate with rainfall.

faqs.q: What size eavestrough do I need?
faqs.a: Most Ontario homes use 4" or 5" K-style eavestroughs. Homes with large roof catchment areas may require 6" commercial-grade systems. We size properly for your specific roof area and rainfall data.

faqs.q: Do gutter guards really work?
faqs.a: Quality micro-mesh gutter guards significantly reduce maintenance — eliminating full-debris clogs entirely. We recommend and install products that allow water in while keeping leaves and pine needles out.

title: Venting Upgrades
tagline: Proper ventilation extends roof life and prevents ice dams.
intro1: Poor attic ventilation is one of the leading causes of premature roof failure, ice dam formation, and elevated heating and cooling costs. A properly ventilated attic maintains temperature balance, reduces moisture buildup, and dramatically extends the life of your roof shingles.
intro2: We assess your current ventilation intake and exhaust balance and recommend the right upgrades — ridge vents, soffit vents, power attic ventilators, or bathroom exhaust fan rerouting — to bring your home to code and optimal performance.
metaTitle: Roofing Ventilation Upgrades Ontario | Top Design Renovation — Free Estimate
metaDesc: Attic ventilation upgrades in Ontario. Ridge vents, soffit vents & power attic ventilators. Prevent ice dams, extend roof life. Free assessment.
whyUs: Full ventilation intake/exhaust balance assessment
Ridge vent, soffit vent & power ventilator installation
Bathroom exhaust fan rerouting to exterior
Ice dam prevention strategies included

benefits.title: Ice Dam Prevention
benefits.desc: Balanced attic ventilation keeps the roof deck at ambient temperature, preventing the heat differential that causes ice dams along eaves.

benefits.title: Energy Savings
benefits.desc: A properly ventilated attic reduces summer cooling loads by 10–15% by expelling heat before it transfers into living spaces.

benefits.title: Extended Roof Life
benefits.desc: Excess attic heat accelerates shingle granule loss and adhesive breakdown. Proper ventilation can add 5–10 years to a roof's lifespan.

faqs.q: How much does attic ventilation upgrading cost?
faqs.a: A standard ventilation upgrade including ridge vent installation and soffit vent improvements costs $1,500–$4,000 for most homes. Power ventilator installation adds $600–$1,200.

faqs.q: What causes ice dams?
faqs.a: Ice dams form when heat escaping from a poorly insulated or under-ventilated attic melts snow on the upper roof, which runs down and refreezes at the cold eaves. The result is water backing up under shingles.

faqs.q: How do I know if my attic ventilation is inadequate?
faqs.a: Signs include ice dams in winter, excessive heat in upper floors in summer, frost on attic framing in cold weather, shingles blistering or curling prematurely, and bathroom exhaust fan condensation issues.

faqs.q: Can ventilation issues cause mould in the attic?
faqs.a: Yes — moisture from bathroom fans, kitchen exhausts, or condensation that are improperly vented into the attic rather than to the exterior can cause significant mould growth. We diagnose and remedy all moisture sources.

title: Roof Inspections
tagline: Know the true condition of your roof before it becomes an emergency.
intro1: A professional roof inspection gives you an accurate picture of your roof's current condition and remaining lifespan — before you're dealing with a leak during a rainstorm. We provide detailed written reports with photos documenting every finding.
intro2: Our inspections are commonly requested before home purchases, after severe storms, for insurance claims, and as part of regular maintenance programs. We're independent — our inspection recommendation is based on what's best for your home, not on selling you a new roof.

benefits.title: Detailed Report

benefits.title: Full Inspection Scope

benefits.title: Insurance Support
benefits.desc: Our inspection reports are accepted by most insurance companies for storm damage claims and are useful documentation for property transactions.

faqs.q: How much does a roof inspection cost in Ontario?
faqs.a: A professional roof inspection with written report typically costs $150–$300. This fee is credited toward any repair or replacement work we complete. Pre-purchase inspections are commonly completed within 48 hours.

faqs.q: When should I get a roof inspection?
faqs.a: We recommend inspections after major hail or wind storms, before purchasing a home, if you notice signs of leakage inside, if your roof is 15+ years old, or every 3–5 years as preventive maintenance.

faqs.q: What does a roof inspection include?
faqs.a: Our inspection covers all roofing surfaces, flashing at all penetrations and transitions, ridge and hip condition, gutters and downspouts, soffit and fascia condition, attic access assessment, and photos of all findings.

faqs.q: Can I use your inspection report for an insurance claim?
faqs.a: Yes. Our detailed photo reports with written assessments documenting storm damage or deterioration are accepted by insurance adjusters. We can also meet with adjusters on-site if needed.

title: Roof Snow Removal
tagline: Protect your roof from dangerous snow and ice loads.
intro1: Heavy snow accumulation on roofs can cause structural failure, ice dam formation, and serious leaking. Ontario winters regularly bring snow loads that exceed the capacity of older roof structures — professional removal protects both your home and your family.
intro2: We provide safe, professional roof snow removal using proper tools that remove snow without damaging shingles or flashing. We also offer seasonal snow removal contracts for peace of mind throughout winter.
metaTitle: Roof Snow Removal Ontario | Top Design Renovation — Fast & Safe
metaDesc: Professional roof snow removal in Ontario. Safe tools that protect your shingles. Emergency & seasonal contracts available. Call (613) 698-1654.
whyUs: Non-abrasive tools — zero shingle damage
Emergency same-day response available
Seasonal maintenance contracts available
Ice dam treatment and steam removal

benefits.title: Structural Protection
benefits.desc: Heavy wet snow can load a roof with 20+ lbs per sq ft. We safely reduce loads before they threaten your roof structure.

benefits.title: Ice Dam Removal
benefits.desc: We safely remove ice dams using controlled steam and mechanical methods without damaging shingles — stopping the leak at its source.

benefits.title: Seasonal Contracts
benefits.desc: Pre-arrange a seasonal snow removal contract and we'll monitor conditions and respond automatically after significant snowfall events.

faqs.q: When is roof snow removal necessary?
faqs.a: Consider professional snow removal when accumulation exceeds 12–18 inches, when you see signs of sagging or hear unusual creaking, when ice dam icicles form at eaves, or after freezing rain events that add significant ice weight.

faqs.q: How much does roof snow removal cost in Ontario?
faqs.a: Single-visit roof snow removal typically costs $200–$600 depending on roof size, accessibility, and snow depth. Seasonal contracts provide better value for homeowners who want ongoing coverage.

faqs.q: Can snow removal damage my shingles?
faqs.a: Only if done improperly with metal shovels or excessive force. Our team uses roof rakes and non-abrasive tools designed for safe snow removal — leaving a 2" snow layer on the deck to protect shingles.

faqs.q: Do you remove ice dams?
faqs.a: Yes. We remove ice dams using controlled steam or careful mechanical methods that eliminate the ice buildup without harming shingles or flashing — stopping the leak source immediately.

title: Interlock & Paving Stones
alt: Interlock driveway and paving stones installation in Ontario
tagline: Elegant outdoor spaces built to last Ontario winters.
intro1: Interlocking stone is one of the highest-impact upgrades you can make to your home's exterior. Whether you're transforming a cracked driveway, creating a backyard patio, or designing a front walkway that makes a statement — our Ontario interlock team delivers precision installation that holds up through every freeze-thaw cycle.
intro2: We work with all major paving stone brands and offer a full range of design styles: classic brick herringbone, modern large-format slabs, natural cobblestone looks, and custom multi-tone patterns. Every installation starts with a properly engineered and compacted granular base — because in Ontario, the base is everything.
metaTitle: Interlock & Paving Stones Ontario | Top Design Renovation — Free Estimate
metaDesc: Expert interlock and paving stone installation in Ontario. Driveways, patios, walkways, steps & pool surrounds. Custom designs built to last. Free estimate.
whyUs: All styles: herringbone, modern slab, cobblestone & custom patterns
Engineered base designed for Ontario freeze-thaw cycles
Driveways, patios, walkways, steps & pool surrounds
Free design consultation included

benefits.title: Custom Design
benefits.desc: From classic herringbone driveways to contemporary large-format patio slabs — we design interlock layouts that complement your home's architecture and your personal style. All colours and patterns available.

benefits.title: Ontario-Proof Durability
benefits.desc: Our installations start with a deep compacted granular base engineered to handle Ontario's extreme freeze-thaw cycles. Properly installed interlock won't heave, shift, or crack for decades.

benefits.title: Instant Curb Appeal & Value
benefits.desc: A new interlock driveway or patio adds immediate visual impact and measurable property value. Interlock consistently ranks among the highest-ROI exterior upgrades for Canadian homeowners.

faqs.q: How much does interlock driveway installation cost in Ontario?
faqs.a: Interlock driveway installation in Ontario typically costs $15–$35 per sq ft installed, depending on stone type, pattern complexity, and site preparation required. A standard double driveway (500–700 sq ft) runs $8,000–$22,000. We provide a detailed written estimate after a free on-site assessment.

faqs.q: How long does interlock last in Ontario's climate?
faqs.a: Quality interlocking stone installed with a proper compacted granular base lasts 25–40 years in Ontario conditions. The key is the base preparation — a minimum 8–12" deep compacted granular base prevents heaving and shifting through freeze-thaw cycles.

faqs.q: Can existing interlock be repaired or relevelled?
faqs.a: Yes — sunken, heaved, or shifted interlock can often be relevelled and reset rather than fully replaced. We lift the affected stones, re-grade and compact the base, and relay the stones. This is significantly more cost-effective than a full replacement.

faqs.q: What interlock styles work best for Ontario homes?
faqs.a: Classic tumbled brick in charcoal or buff tones are perennially popular for driveways. For patios and backyard spaces, large-format 24×24 concrete slabs in modern grey tones are trending across Ontario. We offer free design consultations with physical samples to help you choose.
